parentQuestion: A patient sustains a traumatic brain injury and afterward becomes impulsive, soc...

scaffolds.q: Different parts of the frontal lobe have different specialized functions.
scaffolds.explain: The frontal lobe contains multiple functional regions: motor cortex (movement), dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (working memory, planning), and orbitofrontal cortex (impulse control, social judgment, decision-making). Damage to different regions produces very different deficits.

scaffolds.q: Impulse control and social judgment are functions of the brain that can be selectively damaged.
scaffolds.explain: Impulse control, emotional regulation, and social decision-making are localized functions — primarily in the orbitofrontal cortex and ventromedial prefrontal cortex. Damage here produces disinhibition while leaving other cognitive functions intact.
